title: Summary logs for UK packaging waste: an overview
body: "What summary logs are   Reprocessors and exporters need to use summary logs to record packaging waste activity. Summary logs are spreadsheets that record data about each load that arrives or leaves your site for each registration or accreditation that you hold.  Purpose   Providing summary logs is part of the record-keeping and reporting requirements in the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging and Packaging Waste) Regulations 2024. It is a condition of registration and accreditation. Read more about the reporting requirements.   For accredited operators, the data you provide in your summary log will be used to calculate the waste balance. You will use the waste balance to issue Packaging Recycling Notes (PRNs) and Packaging Waste Export Recycling Notes (PERNs).    The summary logs will also give the regulators more data to help them target fraud and error, protecting the interests of legitimate industry.  When to submit them   The requirement to record your packaging waste in summary logs applies from 1 January 2026.   Registered-only operators   At a minimum registered operators need to update the log at least quarterly, once you can include all the data for the quarter. This is so you can submit the quarterly report before the 21st day of the month following the end of the quarter.  Accredited operators    At a minimum you need to update the log at least once a month, once you can include all the data for the previous month. This is so you can submit the monthly report on the 21st day of every month with the data for the previous month.    For example, you first need to submit your summary log on 21 February 2026. It must include the data for January 2026.    You could update the summary log multiple times during January and February, or just once between 1 and 21 February, as long as it includes all of the January data by 21 February.  Updating your summary log    You can update the log more often than the minimum reporting requirements if you prefer.  Each time you update the log, edit and re-upload the same spreadsheet. Do not delete any existing data as this could affect the accuracy of your waste balance.  You can edit information you previously submitted in the log. If you edit information submitted in a previous reporting period, you need to resubmit that monthly report.  How to fill in, upload and submit them   Anyone can fill in and submit the summary logs for your organisation.  Download the template you need   There are different summary log templates. You must use the templates provided.   You need to use the correct one for your operating activity. Complete a new spreadsheet for each registration or accreditation you have.  You can download the summary log templates.   There are currently 3 templates available. These templates may change as we receive feedback. We expect future changes to be usability improvements rather than changes to data fields although this is not guaranteed. We will add more templates before the requirement comes into force on 1 January 2026 to cover the different types of operating activity and materials being reprocessed or exported.   Technical information   You must:     use a Microsoft Excel version from 2007 onwards    save the template as an XLSX file    We recommend using the desktop version of Excel.  Keep a back-up copy of the document so you do not lose your data if there are any problems with the file.   If you have technical issues with your summary log, contact the EPR helpdesk....
